advantages: Handles sequential decision-making where each action affects future opportunities—natural for control, games, and dialogue.
Learns from weak, delayed feedback (rewards) without requiring correct labels for every step.
Can discover strategies humans did not specify, including creative solutions in simulators.
Unified framework (MDP, policy, value) connects tabular problems to deep RL and modern RLHF pipelines.
disadvantages: Sample inefficient: many environment interactions may be needed before good policies emerge.
Reward design is hard—mis-specified rewards cause reward hacking and unintended behavior.
Unstable training and high variance compared with supervised learning on fixed datasets.
Safety and evaluation are difficult in real-world deployments where exploration can be costly or risky.
